title: Rotarod test in rats
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2s5de","text":"Protocol for performing the rotarod test in rats. This test evaluated motor coordination and balance, and can be used in rat models with motor deficits, such as models of Parkison's disease. Rats with impaired motor function show a decreased latency to fall from the rotating rod.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Bring cages to the behavioral room for at least 01:00:00 prior to the test for habituation
Habituate rats on the rotarod for 00:05:00 at 5 rpm, place back in case they fall
Test rats for 00:01:00 at 5 rpm
Test rats for 00:01:00 at 10 rpm
Test rats for 00:01:00 at 15 rpm
One day after training, perform baseline measurements
Bring cages to the behavioral room for at least 01:00:00 prior to the test for habituation
Test rats at 4-40 rpm for a total duration of 00:05:00
Test rats at 4-40 rpm for a total duration of 00:05:00
Test rats at 4-40 rpm for a total duration of 00:05:00
At different timepoints during the experiment, evaluate changes in motor performance using the same protocol as during baseline
Bring cages to the behavioral room for at least 01:00:00 prior to the test for habituation
Test rats at 4-40 rpm for a total duration of 00:05:00
Test rats at 4-40 rpm for a total duration of 00:05:00
Test rats at 4-40 rpm for a total duration of 00:05:00
